search_queries_used: site:academic.oup.com/bjd cutaneous warts guideline 2014 plantar wart diagnosis complications
candidate_sources_rejected: cryotherapy, salicylic-acid, curettage, laser, and other treatment recommendations
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: No medicine, procedure, application schedule, treatment interval, or autonomous recommendation was mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults or children with a clinician-suspected or established plantar wart; diabetes, neuropathy, poor perfusion, immunosuppression, pregnancy, and diagnostic uncertainty require qualified assessment.
setting_applicability: UAE primary, podiatry, and dermatology review.
UAE_applicability: The clinical descriptors are transferable, but UAE medicine, procedure, consent, infection-control, podiatry, and referral pathways remain local.
recency_verification: The primary BAD 2014 guideline full text was reviewed on 2026-07-14; material recency limits remain.
superseded_check: No newer BAD cutaneous-warts guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact wart terminology and natural-history section supports documenting a plantar wart review, duration, interval change, pain or functional impact, symptoms, site and persistence without inferring treatment response.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting wart identity, age, lesion site, duration, persistence, multiplicity, immune context, pain, function, and cosmetic impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact diagnosis and complications section supports clinician skin inspection, lesion distribution and relevant immune, pain, bleeding or infection context only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-observed morphology and distribution, diagnostic uncertainty, pain, bleeding, secondary infection, and immunosuppression context without inferring diagnosis or treatment.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact lesion count and dimensions, callus and skin-line findings, pain with pressure, diabetes and neurovascular assessment, previous treatment and response, diagnosis, procedure, medicine, referral, and follow-up timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
